title: Juno stenophylla
taxonomy: Juno stenophylla is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family, Orchidaceae. It is native to Mexico. This species is classified as an epiphytic orchid, meaning it grows on other plants, typically trees, for support. The classification of Juno stenophylla is further divided into the genus Juno, which belongs to the subtribe Areliinae. This classification places Juno stenophylla within the family Orchidaceae and the subfamily Epidendroideae.

intro: Juno stenophylla, also known as Juniperus horizontalis, is a slow-growing, evergreen shrub native to North America, characterized by its low-lying, spreading habit and needle-like foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, urinary tract infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Juno stenophylla include terpenes, flavonoids, and lignans, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Juniper berries, derived from the plant, are commonly used to make teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
